name: Grade 10 Reading — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 10 pathway for comparative analysis, rhetoric, research, and evidence-based interpretation. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 10 knowledge and transferable skills in comparative analysis, rhetoric, research, and evidence-based interpretation.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

units.title: Investigate: comparative analysis, rhetoric, research, and evidence-based interpretation
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of comparative analysis, rhetoric, research, and evidence-based interpretation?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about comparative analysis, rhetoric, research, and evidence-based interpretation.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about comparative analysis, rhetoric, research, and evidence-based interpretation, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 10 task connected to comparative analysis, rhetoric, research, and evidence-based interpretation.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
